titulo: Experts call for restrictions on pet flea treatments that harm UK songbirds
resumen:

Conservationists have called for restrictions on pet flea treatments after research found songbird feathers widely contaminated with substances that can damage the birds’ brains and kill unborn chicks.

Almost every feather sample tested from five common species of UK garden birds contained either permethrin, imidacloprid or fipronil – all insecticides that are banned for agricultural use but still common in pet tick and flea treatments.

titulo: Starmer se disculpa por nombramiento de Mandelson vinculado al caso Epstein en Reino Unido
resumen: El primer ministro británico, Keir Starmer, pidió disculpas públicas ante el Parlamento tras la controversia por nombrar a Peter Mandelson como embajador en Estados Unidos. Según reveló The Guardian, Mandelson había recibido una evaluación negativa de seguridad que no fue considerada por la Cancillería. Starmer afirmó que desconocía esa información y que no lo habría designado de haberla sabido. La oposición lo acusa de negligencia, y tras el escándalo destituyó a Mandelson y a un alto funcionario.

titulo: Escalan tensiones entre EE. UU. e Irán con presión militar y negociaciones en Pakistán

Aviones de la Fuerza Aérea estadounidense se acercaron a una base paquistaní mientras se prevé una nueva ronda de diálogos, aunque persisten dudas sobre la participación iraní. Las conversaciones previas en Islamabad terminaron sin acuerdo, con Teherán acusando a Washington de violar la tregua. El presidnete estadounidense Donald Trump confía en lograr un mejor pacto nuclear, mientras China y otros actores piden retomar el diálogo.

titulo: Elon Musk ignora a la justicia francesa por caso sobre X y Grok
resumen: El magnate Elon Musk no se presentó ante la justicia francesa este 19 de abril. El Tribunal de París había citado al dueño de la plataforma X en el marco de una investigación por la difusión de imágenes de pornografía infantil y el uso de la inteligencia artificial Grok para la creación de montajes de carácter sexual. titulo: EE. UU. intercepta buque iraní en Ormuz y eleva tensiones en plena escalada
resumen: Las tensiones aumentaron en el estrecho de Ormuz cuando fuerzas estadounidenses interceptaron el domingo el buque mercante iraní Tuska, en la primera acción de este tipo desde el inicio del bloqueo a puertos iraníes la semana pasada. Según imágenes captadas por un destructor, la embarcación fue atacada tras negarse a obedecer órdenes, lo que dañó su sala de máquinas. Posteriormente, marines abordaron el barco desde helicópteros y tomaron el control. El hecho ocurre tras ataques iraníes a buques en la zona.

titulo: ¿Israel se prepara para ocupar permanentemente parte de Líbano?

Por otro lado, la entrada en vigor del alto el fuego el 17 de abril animó a algunos residentes a regresar a sus hogares.

titulo: Pennsylvania court strikes down ban on use of Medicaid funds for abortions
resumen:
Victory for abortion rights groups as court finds state’s constitution guarantees a right to abortion

A Pennsylvania court on Monday said that the state’s constitution guarantees a right to abortion while striking down a decades-long law banning the use of state Medicaid funds to cover abortion costs.

The ruling by a divided seven-judge panel of the appellate-level commonwealth court is a major victory for Planned Parenthood and abortion clinic operators who first sued Pennsylvania over its Medicaid funding restrictions in 2019.

titulo: Tim Cook to step down as Apple chief as John Ternus named replacement
resumen:
Cook, who will stay on as executive chair, praises head of hardware engineering, who will take over on 1 September

Apple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO after nearly 15 years, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair.

“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release.

titulo: Canadian woman killed after gunman opens fire at Mexico’s Teotihuacán pyramids
resumen:
At least four more injured at world heritage site in latest violent incident as country prepares to co-host World Cup

One Canadian tourist has been killed and at least four other people have been shot after an armed man opened fire at one of Mexico’s most famous tourist destinations, the Teotihuacán pyramids near Mexico City.

The shooting – the latest violent incident to affect Mexico as it prepares to co-host the football World Cup in June – took place on Monday lunchtime and was captured in mobile phone videos shot by visitors to the site.

resumen:
IDF says sledgehammer photo circulating online is authentic and is being viewed with ‘great severity’

The Israeli army has said it is investigating a soldier who was seen apparently striking a statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon.

The Israel Defense Forces said they had determined that an image circulating on social media showing the incident was authentic. The image appears to show an Israeli soldier using a sledgehammer to strike the head of a statue of a crucified Jesus that had fallen off a cross.

titulo: Pro-Russian Rumen Radev on course for runaway victory in Bulgarian elections
resumen:
Stronger-than-expected win for former president could end years of weak coalition governments

The pro-Russian former president Rumen Radev is on course for a runaway victory in Bulgaria’s parliamentary elections, official results show, potentially ending years of weak coalition governments and sidelining long-dominant political forces.

The performance, surpassing opinion polls, is one of the strongest results by a single party in a generation and may end, for now, the instability that led to eight elections in five years.

titulo: Starwatch: Lyrid meteor shower returns to the spring skies
resumen:
First recorded in 687BC, the meteoroids were once part of the tail of a comet discovered in 1861

This week, the annual Lyrid meteor shower returns to the spring skies. Although active since 16 April, the shower peaks during the late evening of Wednesday 22 April and early the next morning.

The chart shows the view looking east from London at 00.01 (BST) on Thursday 23 April. The origin point of the meteors, known as the radiant, is labelled Lyrids. It sits in the constellation of Lyra, the Lyre, close to the bright star of Vega.

The V&A East Storehouse, the National Gallery and an accessible castle in Norwich are among the contenders for this year’s Art Fund museum of the year award, the most prestigious UK prize in the sector.

The annual prize offers the winner £120,000, with £20,000 going to each of the other finalists, who the Art Fund’s director, Jenny Waldman, said had all “innovated in different ways”.

Plath’s husband, Ted Hughes, who would become poet laureate, worked at the zoo briefly as a dish washer, an experience said to have helped fuel his inspiration for The Thought-Fox.

Kiev depende en gran medida del suministro de armamento entregado por Estados Unidos y Europa, pero con la guerra en Irán y la superioridad rusa en fabricación de municiones hacen que no sean suficientes.

titulo: La dirección de À Punt ya preveía una "dana de gran impacto" cuatro días antes de la catástrofe que dejó 230 fallecidos
resumen:
El jefe de Informativos alertó al director general de la cadena, avisado por la responsable de Meteorología, según un mensaje de WhatsApp incoporado a la causa por la jueza instructora

La jueza de la dana cita como testigo al jefe de informat

titulo: La Generalitat trata de proteger el negocio anual de tres millones de la discoteca l'Umbracle de València
resumen: CACSA, "pese a no ser parte directamente afectada por el fallo", presenta un incidente de ejecución en relación con la sentencia que obliga al Ayuntamiento de València a revocar autorizaciones o reubicar festivales para evitar el ruido en la Ciudad de las Artes y las Ciencias

titulo: Iran war live: Hormuz Strait shut down again over US ‘piracy’, says Tehran
resumen: President Trump says US naval blockade will 'remain in full force' until 'our transaction with Iran is 100%' done.
